title: Presidential Award for Excellence in Science, Mathematics, and Engineering Mentoring

infobox.key: Presidential Award for Excellence in Science, Mathematics and Engineering Mentoring
infobox.value: (**PAESMEM**) is a Presidential award established by the United States White House in 1995. The program is administered by the [National Science Foundation](/wiki/National_Science_Foundation "National Science Foundation") (NSF) on behalf of the White House [Office of Science and Technology Policy](/wiki/Office_of_Science_and_Technology_Policy "Office of Science and Technology Policy") (OSTP) to reward outstanding mentoring by individuals and organizations.[[1]](#cite_note-1) PAESMEM is the highest national mentoring award bestowed by the White House.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
[Presidential Awards for Excellence in Science, Mathematics and Engineering Mentoring Official site](http://paesmem.net)

["Presidential Awards Honor Science, Mathematics and Engineering Mentoring | NSF – National Science Foundation"](https://www.nsf.gov/news/news_summ.jsp?cntn_id=102914). *www.nsf.gov*. Retrieved 8 September 2018.

["President Honors Excellence in Mentoring"](https://www.nsf.gov/news/news_summ.jsp?cntn_id=104634) (Press release). [National Science Foundation](/wiki/National_Science_Foundation "National Science Foundation"). 16 November 2005. Retrieved 22 September 2019.
["Susan Kauzlarich, University of California, Davis | paesmem"](http://paesmem.net/node/1366). *paesmem.net*. Retrieved 17 March 2019.

[*Morehouse Professors Win White House/National Science Foundation Awards*](http://www.morehouse.edu/newscenter/morehouseprofessorswinwhitehousenationalsciencefoundationawards.html), [Morehouse College](/wiki/Morehouse_College "Morehouse College"), 2 July 2018, retrieved 7 October 2018
["Overtoun Jenda"](https://paesmem.net/paesmemRecognition/awardeeProfile/1193). *Presidential Awards for Excellence in Science, Mathematics and Engineering Mentoring*. Retrieved 24 February 2021.

Wilson, Zakiya (2012), *Hierarchical Mentoring: A Transformative Strategy for Improving Diversity and Retention in Undergraduate STEM Disciplines*, Journal of Science Education and Technology, vol. 21, Springer Science+Business Media, LLC, pp. 148–156, [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1007/s10956-011-9292-5](https://doi.org/10.1007%2Fs10956-011-9292-5)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American mentoring award

The **Presidential Award for Excellence in Science, Mathematics and Engineering Mentoring** (**PAESMEM**) is a Presidential award established by the United States White House in 1995. The program is administered by the [National Science Foundation](/wiki/National_Science_Foundation "National Science Foundation") (NSF) on behalf of the White House [Office of Science and Technology Policy](/wiki/Office_of_Science_and_Technology_Policy "Office of Science and Technology Policy") (OSTP) to reward outstanding mentoring by individuals and organizations.[[1]](#cite_note-1) PAESMEM is the highest national mentoring award bestowed by the White House.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## A Few Selected Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Presidential_Award_for_Excellence_in_Science,_Mathematics,_and_Engineering_Mentoring&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: A Few Selected Recipients")]

* 1996 – [Richard A. Tapia](/wiki/Richard_A._Tapia "Richard A. Tapia"), [Diola Bagayoko](/wiki/Diola_Bagayoko "Diola Bagayoko")
* 1997 – [Geraldine L. Richmond](/wiki/Geraldine_L._Richmond "Geraldine L. Richmond")
* 1998 – [Nina Roscher](/wiki/Nina_Roscher "Nina Roscher"), Armando A. Rodriguez[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* 2000 – [Maria Elena Zavala](/wiki/Maria_Elena_Zavala "Maria Elena Zavala")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* 2003 –
  + Individual – [Linda B. Hayden](/wiki/Linda_B._Hayden "Linda B. Hayden"), [Margaret Werner-Washburne](/wiki/Margaret_Werner-Washburne "Margaret Werner-Washburne")[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6)
  + Organisation – [Karl W. Reid](/wiki/Karl_W._Reid "Karl W. Reid") ([National Society of Black Engineers](/wiki/National_Society_of_Black_Engineers "National Society of Black Engineers"))[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* 2004 -
  + Organization - [Society for Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science](/wiki/Society_for_Advancement_of_Chicanos_and_Native_Americans_in_Science "Society for Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science")[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* 2005 – [Lenore Blum](/wiki/Lenore_Blum "Lenore Blum"), [Rosemary Gillespie](/wiki/Rosemary_Gillespie_(biologist) "Rosemary Gillespie (biologist)"),[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6) [Cheryl B. Schrader](/wiki/Cheryl_B._Schrader "Cheryl B. Schrader")[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* 2007
  + Individual – Jerzy Leszczynski, Kennedy Reed, Kenneth Sajwan, Laura Bottomley, [Lesia Crumpton-Young](/wiki/Lesia_L._Crumpton-Young "Lesia L. Crumpton-Young"), Mary Anne Nelson, [Patricia DeLeon](/wiki/Patricia_DeLeon "Patricia DeLeon"), Steven Oppenheimer[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)
  + Organisation – Medeva Ghee (The Leadership Alliance & The Partnership for Minority Science Education)[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)
* 2008 – [Richard Zare](/wiki/Richard_Zare "Richard Zare"),[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6) [Susan M. Kauzlarich](/wiki/Susan_M._Kauzlarich "Susan M. Kauzlarich")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* 2009 – [Maja Matarić](/wiki/Maja_Matari%C4%87 "Maja Matarić")[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6)
* 2011 – [Juan E. Gilbert](/wiki/Juan_E._Gilbert "Juan E. Gilbert"), [Jo Handelsman](/wiki/Jo_Handelsman "Jo Handelsman"), [Mary Lou Soffa](/wiki/Mary_Lou_Soffa "Mary Lou Soffa")[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6)
* 2014 – [Erika Tatiana Camacho](/wiki/Erika_Tatiana_Camacho "Erika Tatiana Camacho"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Keivan Stassun](/wiki/Keivan_Stassun "Keivan Stassun")[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* 2015
  + Organisation – [EDGE Foundation](/wiki/EDGE_Foundation "EDGE Foundation"), received by [Ulrica Wilson](/wiki/Ulrica_Wilson "Ulrica Wilson")[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* 2020 - [Overtoun Jenda](/wiki/Overtoun_Jenda "Overtoun Jenda")[[15]](#cite_note-jenda-15)

Full list of awardees, both individuals and organizations, can be found at [[1]](https://nsf-gov-resources.nsf.gov/files/PAESMEM-Awardees.xlsx?VersionId=lIznX6WGdMIP5Un.8yK9WbMPlaivpr.V). The year given above represents the year of the award competition although often the ceremony to receive the award occurred 2-3 years later.

## STEM mentoring

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Presidential_Award_for_Excellence_in_Science,_Mathematics,_and_Engineering_Mentoring&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: STEM mentoring")]

[Mentoring](/wiki/Mentoring "Mentoring") in the [STEM fields](/wiki/STEM_fields "STEM fields") has been shown to assist with student retention, a significant problem in the [STEM pipeline](/wiki/STEM_pipeline "STEM pipeline").[[16]](#cite_note-16)
